frameworks.name: penumbra of learning
frameworks.one_line: change in one variable informs expected change rate in others

frameworks.name: synaptic tagging and capture
frameworks.one_line: neural-level analog where strong plasticity rescues weak plasticity

frameworks.name: normative computational theory of learning
frameworks.one_line: Bayesian-style account linking correlated change to belief updating

core_question: Do humans exploit correlations between variables so that observed change in one rescues learning about another?
key_assumptions: genuine environmental changes are correlated across variables
expectations should update only when genuine change is inferred
individual variables are unreliable indicators of change
manipulations: presence vs absence of a co-occurring change cue around the learning task
strength of the to-be-learned signal in a number prediction task
key_findings: weak learning of a task can be rescued by novelty exposure before or after it
human number prediction shows behavioral signatures consistent with the penumbra account
interpretations: correlated change provides cross-variable information that gates expectation updating
behavioral tagging at the cognitive level parallels synaptic tagging at the neural level
